scenario: A new poetry magazine has asked its readers for submissions, and you decided to submit two of your poems. However, you had a problem using the online submission form, and you are not certain that your submissions were received.
direction: Write an email to the editor of the magazine. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell the editor what you like about the new magazine
Describe the problem you experienced
Ask about the status of your submissions
subject: Problem using submission form

scenario: Your coworker, Kevin, recently recommended a new restaurant for your team to visit. You took the team there, but everyone was disappointed. The food was not as good as expected, and the service was slow.
direction: Write an email to Kevin. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what was wrong with the restaurant
Describe the team's reaction to the visit
Suggest alternative lunch arrangements
subject: Team Lunch Experience

scenario: You are planning a business trip to another city. Your colleague, Maria, recently traveled to the same city for work. You want to ask her for recommendations for places to eat and things to do during your free time.
direction: Write an email to Maria. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are traveling to the city
Ask for her recommendations about restaurants and activities
Mention what you would most like to do and what you would like to avoid
subject: Recommendations for Upcoming Business Trip

scenario: You have been working on a group project with several classmates for a course. One of your group members, Alex, has not been participating actively and has missed several assignments. You want to address this issue and find a solution.
direction: Write an email to Alex. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the current progress of the group project
Explain how his lack of participation is affecting the group
Suggest ways he can contribute to the project moving forward
subject: Group Project Participation

scenario: You are a member of the neighborhood association, and you have noticed that the local park needs some maintenance. You decide to contact the association president, Mr. Williams, to discuss the issues and offer a suggestion.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Williams. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the maintenance issue you have observed in the park
Explain why it is important to address the issue
Make a suggestion for how to deal with the issue
subject: Concern about park maintenance

scenario: You are a student organizing a study group for an upcoming exam. You want to invite your classmates to join the group and explain to them your ideas about how the group should proceed.
direction: Write an email to your classmates. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the purpose of the study group
Describe the topics you plan to cover and your ideas for the sessions
Suggest a time and location for the first meeting
subject: Invitation to join study group

scenario: You recently attended a workshop on digital marketing and found it to be very informative. You want to thank the presenter, Ms. Clark, and ask for additional resources to further your understanding of the topic.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Clark. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for the workshop
Provide a detailed explanation of why you found the workshop to be informative
Ask for additional resources or recommendations for further study
subject: Appreciation and request for additional resources

scenario: You are a university student working on a group project for one of your classes. One of your group members, Jake, has not been contributing to the project and has missed several meetings. This is affecting the progress of the project, and you need to address the issue with him.
direction: Write an email to Jake. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the importance of the project
Explain how his absence has affected the group's progress
Suggest ways he can help the group
subject: Need your contribution to the group project

scenario: Your friend Emma has recently moved to a new city and is feeling a bit overwhelmed. She has reached out to you for advice on how to adjust to her new environment and make new friends. You have lived in several cities before, and you have experience with finding ways to settle into new places.
direction: Write an email to Emma. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how you adjusted to a new city
Give Emma suggestions on how she can make new friends
Offer different ways to help her if she needs more advice
subject: Tips for settling into a new city

scenario: You recently attended a concert by your favorite band at a local venue. You want to thank the event organizer, Mr. Harris, for arranging the event and provide feedback on what you enjoyed most.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Harris. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank him for organizing the concert
Describe what you enjoyed most about the event
Suggest any improvements for future events
subject: Feedback on recent concert

scenario: You are a student who has been working on a research project and need to request an extension on the deadline from your professor, Dr. Jones. You have encountered some unexpected challenges that have delayed your progress.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Jones. In your email, do the following:
goals: Ask for an extension on the deadline
Provide details about the challenges you have encountered
Suggest a new deadline and offer to provide updates on your progress
subject: Request for extension on research project deadline

scenario: You have recently moved into a new apartment and are experiencing issues with the heating system. You need to contact the property manager, Ms. Green, to report the problem and request a repair.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Green. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issues you are experiencing with the heating system
Explain how these issues are affecting your daily life
Request a repair and ask for a timeline for when it will be fixed
subject: Heating system repair request

scenario: You are a student starting a new semester, and you would like some additional information about a course you are interested in that is taught by Dr. Adams. You want to ensure that the course is suitable for you.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Adams. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are interested in the course
Ask for the additional information you would like to have about the course
Thank Dr. Adams for taking the time to respond
subject: Request for additional course information

scenario: You are in Professor Kim's sociology class. Your midterm paper was graded from an old draft because of a website upload error, and the score is much lower than expected. You want to explain the situation and ask for a chance to resubmit.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened with your midterm submission
Describe how the current grade affects your course standing
Request permission to submit the correct version of your paper
subject: Midterm paper submission error

scenario: You accepted a volunteer position at a local clinic through the university service portal, but the hours you have completed are not showing in the system. Your scholarship requires verified community service records by the end of the month.
direction: Write an email to the program supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the issue with your missing volunteer hours
Ask about the verification process and timeline
Suggest how your completed shifts could be documented
subject: Missing volunteer hours in the system

scenario: You were invited to interview for a part-time campus job at the student center. However, the proposed interview time overlaps with a required speaking exam that you cannot reschedule. You are still very interested in the position.
direction: Write an email to the hiring coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you have with the interview time
Ask whether an alternative interview slot is available
Reconfirm your interest in the position
subject: Interview scheduling conflict

scenario: Your dorm room Wi-Fi has been experiencing frequent outages over the past two weeks. You have a timed online exam next week that cannot be paused once started, and you are worried about connectivity issues during the test.
direction: Write an email to the IT Help Desk. In your email, do the following:
goals: Report the connectivity issues and how often they occur
Explain why the situation is urgent because of your upcoming exam
Ask what solutions or backup options are available before your test date
subject: Urgent Wi-Fi connectivity issue in dorm

scenario: Your neighbor, Tom, has been playing loud music late at night for the past week, and it has been difficult for you to sleep. You have an important job interview early next Monday morning and need to rest well this weekend.
direction: Write an email to Tom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the noise issue and how it has been affecting you
Explain why this weekend is particularly important for you
Suggest a reasonable solution that works for both of you
subject: Noise concern from next door

scenario: You ordered a laptop online two weeks ago for your coursework, but when it arrived, the screen had a visible crack. You contacted customer service by phone but were told to submit a written request for a replacement.
direction: Write an email to customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issue with the product you received
Explain how the situation is affecting your ability to complete your coursework
Request a replacement or refund and ask about the expected timeline
subject: Replacement request for damaged laptop

scenario: You recently attended a career fair at your university and had a conversation with a recruiter from a technology company. The recruiter encouraged you to apply for their summer internship program and gave you her business card.
direction: Write an email to the recruiter, Ms. Rivera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Remind her of your conversation at the career fair
Express your interest in the summer internship program
Ask about the application process and any deadlines you should be aware of
subject: Follow-up on summer internship opportunity
